title: VisOnlyQA: Large Vision Language Models Still Struggle with Visual Perception of Geometric Information
abstract: We introduce VisOnlyQA, a benchmark evaluating visual perception of geometric information across 12 task types. We evaluate 23 state-of-the-art LVLMs and show that models often cannot accurately perceive basic geometric information, while human performance is nearly perfect. Fine-tuning on VisOnlyQA training data is not always effective, even for in-distribution tasks.

claims.statement: state-of-the-art LVLMs, including GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5 Pro, perform poorly in the basic geometric perception tasks in VisOnlyQA (48.8% and 79.0% in accuracy on the Real split), while human performance is nearly perfect (93.5%).

This finding suggests that language models play a crucial role in processing visual information encoded by ViT, and strong language models are needed even in geometric perception tasks that do not involve challenging reasoning or knowledge.
